setting.note: Toole's New Orleans is a hot, cluttered comic stage: a medievalist crank in a hunting cap wages war on the modern world from his mother's shotgun house Uptown out to the department-store clock on Canal Street, the neon of Bourbon Street, the French Quarter where he peddles hot dogs, and a failing pants factory down by the river. The map runs from Uptown across the Central Business District and French Quarter to Bywater.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. John Kennedy Toole, first published 1980. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A Confederacy of Dunces, John Kennedy Toole (1980). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A wrought-iron green, the colour of French Quarter balconies and shuttered Uptown porches. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
